Label: Earth
Description: The only planet known to support life, with oceans and land.
Reference: De Revolutionibus Orbium Coelestium, Nicolás Copérnico

Label: Jupiter
Description: Massive gas giant with a Great Red Spot storm.

Label: Mars
Description: Red, dusty, and potentially home to ancient water.

Label: Mercury
Description: Small, rocky, and closest to the Sun

Label: Neptune
Description: Cold, blue ice giant with strong winds.
Reference: Urbain LeVerrier, “Account of the Discovery of the Planet of Le Verrier at Berlin”, Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, Vol. 7, No. 9, pp. 153–157.

Label: Pluto
Description: A small, icy dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, with a highly elliptical orbit.
Reference: Tombaugh, Clyde W.; «The Search for the Ninth Planet, Pluto.» Astronomical Society of the Pacific Leaflets, Vol. 5, No. 209 , pp. 73-80.

Label: Saturn
Description: Gas giant with prominent, beautiful rings.

Label: Uranus
Description: Ice giant tilted on its side, with faint rings.
Reference: Herschel, William; «Account of a Comet. By Mr. Herschel, F. R. S.; communicated by Dr. Watson, Jun. of Bath, F. R. S.» Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society of London, Vol. 71, pp. 492-501.

Label: Venus
Description: Hot, with thick clouds and extreme greenhouse effect.
